mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production signals expanding supply-side capacity and corporate revenue growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO increases coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor demand and falling household income, which causes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of an elevated federal funds rate and a high level of consumer price inflation creates a joint discount-rate and margin-compression effect that generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to achieve a t-statistic > 2.0 in predicting 60-day returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts during a late-cycle expansion signals an impending collapse in construction activity and durable goods demand, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines in late-cycle expansion are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate of more than 0.25 percentage points within one month signals an accelerating monetary easing cycle that compresses the equity discount rate, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) after FEDFUNDS declines > 0.25pp in 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration of real GDP growth during a post-shock recovery period signals a rebound in aggregate demand and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth surprises in post-shock recovery coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously falling industrial production and rising unemployment generate a joint supply-demand contraction that amplifies downside risk to corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of declining INDPRO and rising UNRATE fails to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of joint-signal observations over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales signals strong consumer demand and revenue expansion for large-cap firms, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month RSAFS growth coincides with negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.
